questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Direction : Study the following question carefully and choose the right answer.
In a certain code COURSE is written as ESRUOC. How can BREATH be written in that code?
questions.solution:
C O U R S E   =  E S R U O C

Letters have been written in reverse order

Similarly;

B R E A T H  =  H T A E R B
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: Study the following information carefully and answer the questions given below:

In a certain code language,

‘good time to buy’ is written as ‘sy bo nj kw’

‘invest money and time’ is written as ‘sy ta ge mr’.

‘buy good stuff only’ is written as ‘kw bo rd fp’.

‘only work and money’ is written as ‘ta fp mr ux’.
What is the code for “to” in the given code language?
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Direction : Study the following question carefully and choose the right answer.
If GARMENT is written as 202691422137, how is INDULGE written in that code?
questions.solution:
In this question we have to count places of alphabets from the end point Z.

Suppose Z = 1, Y = 2, X = 3 ……. A = 26.

Then,

GARMENT is written as ⇒20, 26, 9, 14, 22, 13, 7

Similarly,

INDULGE ⇒ 18, 13, 23, 6, 15, 20, 22
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Direction : Study the following question carefully and choose the right answer.
If H = 8 and HAT = 29, find how BOX = ?
questions.solution:
H = 8: Position number in English alphabet

H A T 
↓ ↓ ↓ 
8 + 1 + 20 = 29

Therefore,
 
B O X 
↓ ↓ ↓ 
2 + 15 + 24 = 41
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: Study the following information carefully and answer the questions given below:

In a certain code language ‘Indian banks are associates’ is written as ‘za pn ka sh’, ‘SBI has asscociates banks’ is written as ‘pn za bi ti’, ‘national and Indian institute’ is written as ‘na ha sh sn’ and ‘national has international banks’ is written as ‘bi na mn pn’.
How is ‘banks’ written in that code language?
questions.solution: On comparing the word and their respective codes from S1, S2 and S4 we get code of ‘banks’ is ‘pn’.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.q:
Direction : Stuy the folloiwng question carefully and choose the right answer.
If each of the letters in the English alphabet is assigned an even numerical value by giving A = 2, B = 4 and so on. What would be the total value of the letters for the word LADY when similarly coded?
questions.solution:
L⇒12×2= 24
A⇒01×2=02
D⇒04×2=08
Y⇒25×2=50

Total of numbers 
=
84
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Direction : Stuy the folloiwng question carefully and choose the right answer.
If DRIVER = 12, PEDESTRIAN = 20, EGG = 6, then SHOP = ?
questions.solution:
Clearly, each word is coded by the numeral which is twice the number of letters in the word.
 
So, SHOP = (Number of letters in SHOP) × 2 = 4 × 2 = 8.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.q:
Direction : Stuy the folloiwng question carefully and choose the right answer.
If each of the letters in the English alphabet is assigned odd numerical value beginning A = 1, B = 3 and so on, what will the total value of the letters for the word ‘HOTEL’?
questions.solution:
A ⇒ 1 × 2 – 1 = 1

B ⇒ 2 × 2 – 1 = 3

Therefore,

H⇒08×2 – 1=15
O⇒15×2 – 1=29
T⇒20×2 – 1=39
E⇒05×2 – 1= 09
L⇒12×2 – 1 23

Total of numbers 
=
115
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.q:
Direction : Stuy the folloiwng question carefully and choose the right answer.
If the word LEADER is coded as 20-13-9-12-13-26, how would you write LIGHT?
questions.solution:
L⇒12+8=20
E⇒5+8=13
A⇒1+8=09
D⇒4+8=12
E⇒5+8=13
R⇒18+8=26

Therefore,
 
L⇒12+8=20
I⇒9+8=17
G⇒7+8=15
H⇒8+8=16
T⇒20+8=28

So, 20-17-15-16-28.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.q:
Direction : Stuy the folloiwng question carefully and choose the right answer.
If O = 16, FOR = 42, then what is BACK equal to?
questions.solution:
We have: A = 2, B = 3, ……..Z = 27 then FOR = F + O + R = 7 + 16 + 19 = 42

BACK = B + A + C + K = 3 + 2 + 4 + 12 = 21.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Direction : Stuy the folloiwng question carefully and choose the right answer.
If D = 4 and FAT = 9, how will you code PIN = ?
questions.solution:
We have: A = 1, B = 2, C = 3, …….Y = 25, Z = 26.

Clearly, the code for a word is obtained by dividing the sum of the individual value of its letters by the number of letters in the word.

Thus FAT = (F + A + T )/3 = (6 + 1 + 20)/3

= 27/3 = 9

So, PIN = (P + I + N)/3 = (16 + 9 + 14)/3 = 39/3 = 13
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.q:
Direction : Study the following question carefully and choose the right answer.
If A = 26, SUN = 27, Then MOON = ?
questions.solution:
Here, each letter is represented by numeral denoting its position from the end of the English alphabet.

i.e. Z = 1, Y = 2, ……..M = 14,….., C = 24, B = 25, A = 26.

Then SUN = 8 + 6 + 13 = 27

MOON = 14 + 12 + 12 + 13 = 51.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

Direction : Study the following question carefully and choose the right answer.
If A = 2, M = 26, Z = 52, then RAT = ?
questions.solution:
Clearly, each letter is assigned a numerical value which is twice the numeral denoting its positing in English alphabet.

So RAT = R + A + T

              = 18 × 2 + 2 + 20 × 2

              = 36 + 2 + 40 = 78.

Direction : Study the following question carefully and choose the right answer.
If certain language CHARCOLE coded as 45164937 and MORALE is coded as 296137, how is the word ALLOCHRE coded in that language?
questions.solution:
Clearly, this is a question of direct-coding. Thus, the individual codes are as under :
 
   Letter C H A R O L M E
   Code 4 5 1 6 9 3 2 7
 
Thus, the code for ALLOCHRE is 13394567.

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Direction : Study the following question carefully and choose the right answer.
In certain code RAIN is written as 8$%6 and MORE is wriiten as 7#8@. How REMAIN written in that code ?
questions.solution:
Clearly, no aaparent rule of coding is visible in the given codes. Also, the given words have 'R' in common and '8' in common at the corresponding places in the code. This implies that letters have been assigned set numeral/ symbol codes i.e. this is a question of direct-coding .
Thus, we may sort out the individual codes as under :

   Letter  R  A    I  N    M   O    E
   Code  8  $   %   6    7   #    @
 
Thus, the codes for REMAIN is 8@7$%6.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Direction : Study the following question carefully and choose the right answer.
In a coding system PEN is written as NZO and BARK as CTSL. How can we write PRANK in that coding system?
questions.solution:
PEN
NZO

BARK
CTSL

Therefore;
 
PRANK
NSTOL
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Direction : Study the following question carefully and choose the right answer.
In a certain language, BUTTER is coded as CVUUFS, BREAD is coded as CSFBE, the how COFFEE is coded?
questions.solution:
BUTTER
↓↓↓↓↓↓
+1+1+1+1+1+1
↓↓↓↓↓↓
CVUUFS

BREAD
↓↓↓↓↓
+1+1+1+1+1
↓↓↓↓↓
CSFBE

Similarly;
 
COFFEE
↓↓↓↓↓↓
+1+1+1+1+1+1
↓↓↓↓↓↓
DPGGFF
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: Study the following information carefully and answer the questions given below:

In a certain code language ‘Indian banks are associates’ is written as ‘za pn ka sh’, ‘SBI has asscociates banks’ is written as ‘pn za bi ti’, ‘national and Indian institute’ is written as ‘na ha sh sn’ and ‘national has international banks’ is written as ‘bi na mn pn’.
‘international’ is written as
questions.solution: On comparing the word and their respective codes from S2, S3 and S4 we get code of ‘international’ is ‘mn’.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
